patch.value: fix: nightly windows/Node24 red — vcs-extension spec asserts POSIX 'relationships/events.jsonl' against native default store path
vcs-extension.spec.ts:402 asserts via path.join so windows leg passes; nightly windows/Node24 red reduces to the pm-hvt3 spec only

Nightly run 29676618564 (2026-07-19): windows/Node24 leg red on TWO specs — the known pm-hvt3 extension-copy-safety spec plus a NEW failure in tests/unit/packages/vcs-extension.spec.ts ("executes every source command and guarded failure boundary through the host SDK context"). macOS leg carries only the hvt3 spec.

Root cause: line 402 (introduced by PR#605, workspace-transactions lane) asserts `defaultPathStore.path` contains the POSIX literal "relationships/events.jsonl"; on Windows the relationship event store default path renders with backslashes, so `toContain` fails. Product behavior is correct (native separators); this is a spec-side portability defect — same class as closed pm-u5zr (POSIX endsWith), pm-lt6n, pm-s5pe, pm-i84i.

Fix: assert via `path.join("relationships", "events.jsonl")` (spec already imports node:path). Windows leg goes fully green only together with pm-hvt3.

value.note: line 402 POSIX assertion fixed to path.join

patch.value: Fixed vcs-extension.spec.ts:402 to assert path.join('relationships','events.jsonl') instead of a POSIX literal.
windows/Node24 nightly leg failure surface reduces to the pm-hvt3 extension-copy-safety spec only.
Spec passes locally (5/5); linux suite unaffected; windows verification via next nightly run.
Spec-side portability fix shipped on main: assertion now uses path.join, matching native separators on Windows.
